statement: The Sun is a light source.
explanation: True! The Sun makes its own light, which makes it our most important light source.

statement: A mirror is a light source because it shines.
explanation: False! A mirror only reflects light from other things. It does not make its own light.

statement: We can see things because light bounces off them and into our eyes.
explanation: True! Light travels from a source, hits an object, and reflects into our eyes so we can see it.

statement: If you are in a completely dark room, your eyes will adjust and you can see perfectly.
explanation: False! Without any light at all, it is impossible to see. Our eyes need light to work.

statement: Opaque materials let all light pass through them.
explanation: False! Opaque materials block all light. You cannot see through them.

statement: Glass is a transparent material.
explanation: True! Transparent materials let light pass through them clearly.

statement: A wooden door is translucent.
explanation: False! A wooden door is opaque because it blocks all light from passing through.

statement: If a material is translucent, you can see light through it but not clear shapes.
explanation: True! Translucent things (like frosted glass) let some light through but scatter it, making shapes blurred.

statement: The Moon is the biggest light source in the night sky.
explanation: False! The Moon doesn't make its own light. It only reflects light from the Sun.

statement: A candle flame is a light source.
explanation: True! A burning candle produces its own light.

statement: Light travels in squiggly, wavy lines.
explanation: False! Light always travels in perfectly straight lines.

statement: Shadows happen because light cannot bend around objects.
explanation: True! Because light travels in straight lines, when an object blocks it, a dark shadow forms behind it.

statement: A ray diagram uses straight arrows to show how light travels.
explanation: True! We draw straight arrows in a ray diagram to represent the straight path of light rays.

statement: If you look through a bent tube, you can still see the light at the other end.
explanation: False! Light cannot bend around the curve of the tube, so it will not reach your eye.

statement: If you move a torch closer to a toy, the toy's shadow on the wall gets smaller.
explanation: False! Moving the light source closer makes it block more light angles, so the shadow gets LARGER.

statement: In a ray diagram, the arrow always points away from the light source.
explanation: True! The arrow shows the direction the light is travelling: away from the source.

statement: If you line up three holes in three cards, you can see a light through them.
explanation: True! Because light travels in a straight line, it can pass straight through all three aligned holes.

statement: A shadow is always the exact same size as the object.
explanation: False! A shadow changes size depending on how close the object is to the light source.

statement: Transparent objects cast very dark, sharp shadows.
explanation: False! Transparent objects let light through, so they cast very faint shadows or no shadow at all.

statement: A solar eclipse happens because the Moon blocks light travelling in a straight line to Earth.
explanation: True! The Moon casts a shadow on Earth because it blocks the straight path of the Sun's light.

statement: Reflection happens when light passes straight through a window.
explanation: False! Reflection is when light hits a surface and BOUNCES back.

statement: Mirrors are great at reflecting light because they are smooth and shiny.
explanation: True! Smooth, shiny surfaces reflect light evenly, giving a clear reflection.

statement: A bumpy, rough surface reflects light perfectly like a mirror.
explanation: False! Rough surfaces scatter the light in many different directions, so there is no clear image.

statement: Cyclists wear reflective vests so they can glow in the dark like fireflies.
explanation: False! The vests don't glow themselves. They just reflect the light from car headlights.

statement: A piece of white paper reflects light, but we can't see our face in it because its surface is rough.
explanation: True! Paper reflects light, which is why we can see it, but microscopically it's rough, so it scatters the light.

statement: Still water can act like a mirror.
explanation: True! Because the surface of still water is very smooth, it reflects light clearly.

statement: When a lake is very windy and wavy, you can see a perfect reflection of the trees.
explanation: False! The waves make the water surface rough, which scatters the light breaking the reflection.

statement: Periscopes use mirrors to bounce light around corners.
explanation: True! By using two mirrors at different angles, periscopes reflect light so you can see over walls.

statement: Dark, dull materials like black cloth are the best at reflecting light.
explanation: False! Dark, dull materials absorb most light and reflect very little.

statement: If you look at a shiny, curved spoon, your reflection might look upside-down or stretched.
explanation: True! The curved surface bounces the rays of light at different angles, distorting the image.

statement: The Earth is at the exact center of our solar system.
explanation: False! The Sun is at the center of the solar system, and Earth orbits around it.

statement: There are 8 planets in our solar system.
explanation: True! The planets are: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

statement: Mercury is the hottest planet because it is closest to the Sun.
explanation: False! Venus is actually hotter than Mercury because Venus has a thick atmosphere that traps heat.

statement: The Sun is a star.
explanation: True! The Sun is a star, just like the ones we see at night, but it is much closer to Earth.

statement: Planets travel around the Sun in square-shaped paths.
explanation: False! Planets travel in oval-shaped or circular paths called orbits.

statement: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.
explanation: True! Jupiter is a gas giant and is much larger than any other planet.

statement: Neptune takes less time to orbit the Sun than Earth does.
explanation: False! Neptune is much further away, so its orbit takes about 165 Earth years!

statement: Mars looks red because its surface is covered in iron oxide (rust).
explanation: True! The soil on Mars has lots of rusted iron in it, giving it a red color.

statement: Stars make their own light, but planets only reflect star light.
explanation: True! Stars like our Sun are burning balls of gas that make light. Planets just reflect it.

statement: The asteroid belt is located between Earth and Mars.
explanation: False! The asteroid belt is located between Mars and Jupiter.

statement: Earth spinning on its axis causes day and night.
explanation: True! As Earth rotates, the side facing the Sun has daytime, and the side facing away has night.

statement: It takes the Earth about 24 hours to spin around one time.
explanation: True! One full rotation takes 24 hours, which is why our day is 24 hours long.

statement: When it is morning in Vietnam, it is also morning everywhere else in the world.
explanation: False! Because the Earth is round and spins, different parts of the world face the Sun at different times.

statement: Your shadow is longest at midday (noon) when the Sun is overhead.
explanation: False! At midday the Sun is very high, so your shadow is very short. Shadows are longest at sunrise and sunset.

statement: Earth's axis is perfectly straight up and down.
explanation: False! Earth's axis is actually tilted on an angle (about 23.5 degrees), which causes our seasons.

statement: If the Earth stopped spinning, one side would be very hot and one side very cold.
explanation: True! One side would face the Sun constantly and get hot, while the other side would stay dark and freeze.

statement: Earth travels around the Sun, and this journey takes about 365 days.
explanation: True! One full orbit around the Sun takes one Earth year.

statement: Shadows get shorter and shorter during the entire afternoon.
explanation: False! Shadows are shortest at midday, and then they get LONGER as the afternoon goes on towards sunset.

statement: The tilt of the Earth means some countries get more hours of daylight in the summer.
explanation: True! When a part of Earth leans toward the Sun, it stays in the sunlight longer each day.

statement: The Sun literally moves across the sky from East to West every day.
explanation: False! The Sun isn't moving across the sky; the Earth is spinning, making the Sun appear to move.
